figures.caption: Fig. 1. Overview of NeuroSonic. (a) EEG and audio signals are partitioned into patches, {Ei} and {Xj}, and projected through modality-specific encoders fE(·) and fA(·) into a shared latent space for joint modeling. (b) A time-conditioned gated Transformer processes the combined sequence together with a corrupted acoustic state zt, obtained by interpolating clean audio with Gaussian noise ϵ at time t, along the flow-matching path. Adaptive layer normalization and RMS-stabilized attention are used to preserve stable feature scaling across interpolation times. (c) The velocity-based objective trains the predicted velocity vpred, computed from the predicted clean state Xpred, to match the target transport velocity vt governing acoustic transport under EEG conditioning.

Table 1. Objective evaluation of EEG-conditioned speech reconstruction under crosssubject evaluation. Lower values indicate better performance for FAD, LSD, SC, and inference time (seconds). Results are reported as mean ± standard deviation. Best values for each metric are shown in bold.
Table 2. Perceptual evaluation using DNSMOS. Higher values indicate better perceptual quality. Results are reported as mean ± standard deviation. Best results among learned models are shown in bold.
